Guest.title: Guest
Guest.description: Represents a guest registered for the 'Customer Meet 2026' event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Guest entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the guest.

email.description: The email address of the guest.

whatsappNumber.description: The WhatsApp contact number of the guest, including international dialing code.

department.description: The department of the guest within their company.

designation.description: The job designation or title of the guest.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the guest registered.

qrCodeData.description: The raw data encoded in the guest's QR code for check-in.

qrCodeImageUrl.description: URL to the image of the guest's QR code.

checkedIn.description: Indicates whether the guest has checked in to the event (default false).

checkInTime.description: The date and time the guest checked in. Null if not yet checked in.

checkInPhotoUrl.description: URL to a photo taken during the guest's check-in. Null if no photo was taken or guest not checked in.

googleSheetsRowId.description: The unique identifier for the corresponding row in the Google Sheets spreadsheet used for data import/export.

confirmationSent.description: Indicates whether a confirmation message has been sent to the guest (default false).

TeamUser.title: TeamUser
TeamUser.description: Represents an authorized team member who can log into the check-in system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeamUser entity, corresponding to Firebase Authentication user ID (uid).

name.description: The full name of the team member.

email.description: The email address of the team member, used for login.

role.description: The role of the team member (e.g., 'admin' or 'staff').

createdAt.description: The date and time when the team user account was created.

CheckInLog.title: CheckInLog
CheckInLog.description: Records each instance of a guest checking into the event, including details about the check-in process.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CheckInLog entity.

guestId.description: Reference to the Guest who was checked in. (Relationship: Guest 1:N CheckInLog)

guestName.description: The full name of the guest at the time of check-in, for historical logging purposes.

checkInTime.description: The exact date and time the guest checked in.

checkedInBy.description: Reference to the TeamUser who performed the check-in. (Relationship: TeamUser 1:N CheckInLog)

photoUrl.description: URL to a photo taken during this specific check-in event, if applicable.

method.description: The method used for check-in (e.g., 'qr_scan' or 'onsite').

definition.description: Stores details for authorized team members, including their role. This collection serves as the primary source of truth for DBAC (Database-Based Access Control) to determine system permissions. Access is restricted such that only the authenticated user can read their own profile, and creation/updates are typically reserved for administrators (implemented via security rules or backend functions).

params.name: teamUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team member, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains registration information for all event guests. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, allowing any authenticated team member (determined by existence in the 'team_users' collection) to read, create, update, and delete entries. This structure supports efficient queries for the guest list, including sorting by 'registrationDate' in descending order as required.

params.name: guestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a guest registration.

definition.description: Records individual guest check-in events. Each log includes a reference to the guest and the team member who performed the check-in, along with a denormalized guest name for historical integrity. This collection's security posture is homogeneous, granting any authenticated team member read and write access. No authorization-specific denormalization is required within these documents as access is global for authorized team members.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific check-in log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering to the core principles and mandates. Authorization Independence is paramount: instead of relying on hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules, access is determined solely by the presence and role of the authenticated `request.auth.uid` within the `/team_users/{teamUserId}` collection. This eliminates transactional complexities and makes rules atomic and easy to debug. All authorization checks for application data (`guests`, `checkInLogs`) will verify if `request.auth.uid` exists in the `team_users` collection, ensuring only authenticated team members can interact with the system.

Structural Segregation is applied by creating distinct top-level collections for `guests`, `team_users`, and `checkInLogs`. Each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture: all documents within `/guests` are accessible to any authenticated team member, as are all documents within `/checkInLogs`. This simplifies security rules, as there's no need for complex conditional logic within a single collection based on document content (e.g., 'public' vs. 'private' documents).

Queryability and Authorization (QAPs) are supported as follows:
*   For `/team_users/{teamUserId}`, rules will ensure that users can only read their own user profile (i.e., `request.auth.uid == teamUserId`). This naturally supports QAPs for list operations by restricting results to the authenticated user's record.
*   For `/guests/{guestId}` and `/checkInLogs/{logId}`, the security model allows any authenticated team member to read/list all documents in these collections. This is a deliberate design choice based on the application requirements, where all team members have broad access to event management data. Rules will grant `list` access to the entire collection if the `request.auth.uid` is an authenticated team member (verified by `exists(/team_users/$(request.auth.uid))`). This is QAP-compliant as it does not attempt to filter results based on user-specific attributes within the collection itself; rather, it grants or denies access to the *entire* collection for `list` operations, making the rule efficient and predictable.

Data clarity is maintained through explicit schema definitions, consistent naming conventions (e.g., `{guestId}`), and standardized fields. The `Guest` entity includes `registrationDate` which will be used for client-side sorting as requested, by ordering Firestore queries `orderBy('registrationDate', 'desc')` to display newest entries first, facilitating efficient data retrieval for the guest list table and filtered results.
